# Antitrust and Labor Markets

## Learning goals
- Evaluate monopsony power, noncompetes, wage fixing, and no-poach agreements.
- Use matched employer-employee data and vacancy data where available.
- Combine worker narratives with econometric evidence.

## Core topics
- Measuring labor market concentration and labor supply elasticity.
- Event studies around noncompete bans or no-poach disclosures.
- Wage posting data vs. realized wages; vacancy scraping considerations.
- Mobility and retention metrics; heterogeneity by occupation/region.

::: {.callout-note title="Method box"}
- Monopsony elasticities from quit rates and wage responses.
- Diff-in-diff on policy shocks (e.g., state-level noncompete changes).
- Synthetic control for single-state reforms.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Qualitative evidence"}
- HR policies, franchise agreements, and recruiting playbooks.
- Worker interviews and surveys on outside options and mobility barriers.
- Internal memos on wage-setting and coordination risks.
:::

::: {.callout-note title="Citations and comparative note"}
- Cite key US cases and enforcement actions on wage fixing/no-poach and noncompetes; include state-level reforms (e.g., Washington franchise no-poach).
- For comparative context, add EU/UK wage-fixing/no-poach enforcement where emerging, and any guidance from other jurisdictions.
- Reference labor monopsony empirical papers for elasticity estimation and mobility effects.
:::
